chief_complaint: A 10-year-old male patient presented with a 2-day history of fever, headache, and altered mental status.
history: The patient had been experiencing symptoms of COVID-19 for 5 days prior to the onset of neurological symptoms. He had been treated with oseltamivir and azithromycin. The patient's past medical history was significant for asthma and allergies. He had no known allergies to medications.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 38.5°C, blood pressure 90/60 mmHg, pulse 120 bpm, respiratory rate 30 breaths/min. Physical examination revealed a lethargic patient with decreased level of consciousness, bilateral papilledema, and hepatomegaly.
labs: ECG: normal. Blood tests: sodium 130 mmol/L, potassium 4.5 mmol/L, creatinine 2.5 mg/dL, AST 500 U/L, ALT 400 U/L, bilirubin 5 mg/dL. Imaging: MRI brain showed diffuse necrotic lesions in the thalamus, basal ganglia, and brainstem. Liver biopsy showed massive necrosis.
correct_diagnosis: Acute Necrotizing Encephalopathy (ANE) secondary to COVID-19
differentials: Acute Necrotizing Encephalopathy (ANE) secondary to COVID-19
correct_ranking: Acute Necrotizing Encephalopathy (ANE) secondary to COVID-19
